body_txt: This is where someone In-Universe narrates the present (or possibly the future), and when that future eventually becomes the present, the narration in question will be revealed. It's usually stated in past tense, but not always. A Fourth-Wall Observer or Third-Person Person might do this; expect them to be called on it by another character. Can overlap with Posthumous Narration . One especially common instance of this is in court cases, since everything has to be recorded by the stenographer, but they're not allowed to add their own observations. So the judge might say something like " Let the record show the counsel is holding up two fingers, " which the stenographer can record. Note that this is for narration, not a prophecy . Compare Thinking Out Loud . Contrast Present Tense Narrative . Super-Trope of Calling Your Actions .
